narrative: Your law school application was denied because our predictive model, which assesses the likelihood of passing the bar exam on the first try, estimated a 75.8% chance that you would not pass, tipping the scales against admission. This prediction drew heavily from your academic record, particularly your position in Year 3 where you ranked in the second decile among your peers—far below the typical fifth or sixth decile spot of students who went on to pass the bar. That Year 3 standing stood out as the single biggest factor pushing the model toward forecasting failure, as it signaled potential challenges in sustaining strong performance over time. Your Year 1 decile of third place added to this concern, reinforcing a pattern of below-average rankings compared to the sixth decile average for bar passers, making it the second-most influential drag on the prediction. On a brighter note, your LSAT score of 29.3 worked in your favor, pulling the prediction somewhat toward success since it showed solid reasoning ability, even if it fell short of the 37.3 average for those who passed. While your undergraduate GPA of 2.9 and middle family income bracket aligned somewhat with accepted students—where middle income is common at about 35%—they didn't outweigh the ranking issues. Your choice to pursue full-time studies matches the norm for passers, who are overwhelmingly full-time like you. Overall, your profile compared to typical admitted students, who boast higher decile rankings and stronger test scores, placed you in a riskier category for bar success. This isn't a judgment on your potential or background—it's just the model highlighting where patterns from past students suggest hurdles. To move forward, consider retaking the LSAT to boost that positive influence, strengthening your later-year grades through targeted coursework or tutoring, and reapplying with an updated profile that addresses these key areas. Many students in similar positions have improved their standings and succeeded on later attempts. You're capable of shifting this trajectory with focused effort.
